metadata.title: Implement Dexie Database Layer with Schema v1

story.iWant: Dexie.js configured with schema v1 for all tables
story.soThat: I can persist sessions, telemetry, and user data locally with type-safe queries
story.tasks: - Create src/services/storage/db.ts with DiscalculasDB class - Define TypeScript interfaces in src/services/storage/schemas.ts - Create src/services/storage/localStorage.ts with STORAGE_KEYS wrappers - Create src/services/storage/migrations.ts (empty for v1, prepared for future) - Write comprehensive tests for database operations - Test useLiveQuery() integration with React component - Verify IndexedDB in browser DevTools

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
**Given** the folder structure is in place (Story 1.3 complete) **When** I implement the Dexie database layer **Then** src/services/storage/db.ts exports a DiscalculasDB class with these tables:
* sessions (id, timestamp, module, [timestamp+module]) * assessments (id, timestamp, status) * drill_results (id, sessionId, timestamp, module) * telemetry_logs (id, timestamp, event, [timestamp+event]) * magic_minute_sessions (id, sessionId, timestamp) * difficulty_history (id, sessionId, timestamp, module) * experiments (id, status) * experiment_observations (id, experimentId, variantId, timestamp)
**And** TypeScript interfaces defined in src/services/storage/schemas.ts for each table **And** src/services/storage/localStorage.ts exports wrappers for STORAGE_KEYS: * STREAK, LAST_SESSION_DATE, USER_SETTINGS, LAST_USED_MODULE, RESEARCH_MODE_ENABLED
**And** A simple test successfully writes to and reads from sessions table **And** useLiveQuery() from dexie-react-hooks works in a test React component

artifacts.code:
**Completed Stories:**
Story 1.1: Vite project initialized - package.json with dexie@4.2.1 and dexie-react-hooks@4.2.0 - tsconfig.json with strict mode and @/ path alias
Story 1.2: Tailwind CSS + shadcn/ui configured - Design system ready for UI components
Story 1.3: Feature-based folder structure created - src/services/storage/ directory exists and ready for implementation - src/__tests__/ directory for test files
**Existing Relevant Files:**
tsconfig.json (paths configuration): ```json { "compilerOptions": { "strict": true, "baseUrl": ".", "paths": { "@/*": ["src/*"] } } } ```
**No existing database files** - This story creates them from scratch
artifacts.dependencies:
**Production Dependencies:** - dexie@4.2.1 - IndexedDB wrapper with TypeScript support - dexie-react-hooks@4.2.0 - React hooks for live queries (useLiveQuery)
**Development Dependencies:** - vitest@4.0.0 - Test framework - @testing-library/react@16.3.0 - React component testing
**Browser APIs:** - IndexedDB (requires Chrome 90+, Firefox 88+, Safari 14+) - LocalStorage (ubiquitous browser support)
**Related Future Stories:** - Story 1.5: React Context will consume this database layer - Story 1.6: Testing infrastructure will validate database operations - Stories 2+: All features will persist data using this layer

story-context.constraints:
**Technical Constraints:**
1. **100% Test Coverage Mandatory** - All database operations must have unit tests - Integration test for useLiveQuery() hook - Coverage threshold enforced in CI
2. **TypeScript Strict Mode** - No 'any' types in production code - All interfaces must match tech-spec exactly - Dexie Table<T> generic types required
3. **Schema Versioning** - Use this.version(1).stores({ ... }) pattern - Prepare migrations.ts for future schema changes (v2+) - Never modify v1 schema after Epic 1 completion
4. **Singleton Pattern** - Export singleton: export const db = new DiscalculasDB(); - Single database instance across entire app - No new DiscalculasDB() calls in feature code
5. **IndexedDB Quota Management** - Typical browser limit: 50-100 MB - Story 1.4 does NOT implement quota monitoring (deferred to Epic 5) - Prepare for future telemetry pruning
6. **Data Privacy** - userId always "local_user" (no unique identifiers) - No external database connections - All data stored locally only
7. **Error Handling** - Try-catch around all Dexie operations - Graceful fallback if IndexedDB unavailable - LocalStorage validation for malformed JSON
8. **Naming Conventions** - Tables: snake_case, plural (sessions, telemetry_logs) - Interfaces: PascalCase (Session, TelemetryLog) - Files: camelCase.ts (db.ts, schemas.ts, localStorage.ts)
9. **Dependencies Version Lock** - dexie@4.2.1 exactly (no ^4.2.1) - dexie-react-hooks@4.2.0 exactly - Verified compatibility 2025-11-09
10. **Browser Compatibility** - Test on Chrome 90+ (primary target) - IndexedDB required (no graceful degradation in Story 1.4) - Future Story 7.2 will add compatibility warnings

tests.standards:
**Testing Standards (from Epic 1 tech-spec):**
1. **AAA Pattern Mandatory** - Arrange: Set up test data - Act: Execute operation - Assert: Verify results
2. **100% Coverage Required** - Statements: 100% - Branches: 100% - Functions: 100% - Lines: 100%
3. **Co-Located Test Files** - db.test.ts next to db.ts - schemas.test.ts next to schemas.ts - localStorage.test.ts next to localStorage.ts
4. **Test Framework: Vitest** - Use describe() blocks for grouping - Use it() for individual tests - Use beforeEach() for test setup - Use afterEach() for cleanup (close DB)
5. **Integration Tests** - Test useLiveQuery() with React Testing Library - Create test component that uses Dexie hook - Verify reactive updates
6. **Browser Verification** - Manual test: Open browser DevTools - Navigate to Application tab → IndexedDB - Verify DiscalculasDB exists with all tables - Verify test records appear after test runs
tests.locations:
**Test File Locations:**
- src/services/storage/db.test.ts (Dexie database tests) - src/services/storage/schemas.test.ts (Schema validation tests) - src/services/storage/localStorage.test.ts (LocalStorage wrapper tests) - src/services/storage/migrations.test.ts (Migration placeholder tests)
**Integration Test Location:**
- src/__tests__/database-integration.test.tsx (useLiveQuery hook test)
tests.ideas:
**Unit Test Ideas:**
**db.test.ts:**
1. Test Database Initialization - Create DiscalculasDB instance - Verify all 8 tables exist - Verify database name is 'DiscalculasDB' - Verify version is 1
2. Test Sessions Table Write/Read - Write a Session record - Read it back by id - Verify all fields match - Test timestamp index query
3. Test Compound Index Query - Write multiple sessions with different modules - Query by [timestamp+module] compound index - Verify correct records returned
4. Test All Tables CRUD - For each of 8 tables: Create, Read, Update, Delete - Verify auto-increment id works - Verify indexed fields support queries
5. Test Foreign Key Pattern (sessionId) - Create a Session - Create DrillResult with sessionId reference - Query drill_results by sessionId - Verify correct association
6. Test Error Handling - Attempt to write with missing required field - Verify Dexie throws error - Catch and handle gracefully
7. Test Database Health Check - Call ensureDBHealth() - Verify returns true - Check telemetry_logs has health_check record
8. Test Query Performance Monitoring - Call queryWithTiming() with slow query - Verify console.warn called if >100ms - Verify result still returned
**schemas.test.ts:**
1. Test Session Interface Validation - Create valid Session object - Verify TypeScript types compile - Verify optional fields work (confidencePre, confidencePost)
2. Test All Interface Structures - For each interface: Create sample object - Verify required fields - Verify optional fields - Verify TypeScript compilation
3. Test userId Always "local_user" - Create Assessment with userId - Create TelemetryLog with userId - Create ExperimentObservation with userId - Verify all default to "local_user"
**localStorage.test.ts:**
1. Test getUserSettings Default - Clear localStorage - Call getUserSettings() - Verify returns DEFAULT_SETTINGS
2. Test getUserSettings Persistence - Set user settings - Call getUserSettings() - Verify correct values returned
3. Test setUserSettings Partial Update - Set initial settings - Update only reducedMotion - Verify other fields unchanged
4. Test getUserSettings with Invalid JSON - Manually write malformed JSON to localStorage - Call getUserSettings() - Verify returns DEFAULT_SETTINGS (fallback)
5. Test All STORAGE_KEYS Wrappers - Test getStreak() / setStreak() - Test getLastSessionDate() / setLastSessionDate() - Test getLastUsedModule() / setLastUsedModule() - Test getResearchModeEnabled() / setResearchModeEnabled()
6. Test Validation Logic - Pass invalid types to validateUserSettings() - Verify Boolean() coercion works - Verify Number() fallback to default
**Integration Test Ideas:**
**database-integration.test.tsx:**
1. Test useLiveQuery Hook - Create test component using useLiveQuery() - Render component - Verify initial query result - Add record to database - Verify component re-renders with new data
2. Test Reactive Updates - Query sessions table with useLiveQuery() - Add session via db.sessions.add() - Verify hook returns updated list - Delete session - Verify hook returns updated list
3. Test Multiple Live Queries - Create component with 2 useLiveQuery() hooks - Update one table - Verify only relevant hook re-renders
**Browser DevTools Test (Manual):**
1. Open application in browser 2. Open DevTools → Application tab → IndexedDB 3. Verify DiscalculasDB exists 4. Verify all 8 tables visible 5. Run tests (npm run test) 6. Refresh IndexedDB view 7. Verify test records appear in tables 8. Manually inspect record structure 9. Verify indexes exist (timestamp, module, etc.)
**Edge Case Test Ideas:**
1. Test IndexedDB Unavailable - Mock IndexedDB as undefined - Attempt to create db - Verify graceful error handling - (Note: Full implementation deferred to Story 7.2)
2. Test Concurrent Writes - Write to same table from multiple promises - Verify all writes succeed - Verify no data corruption
3. Test Large Dataset - Write 1000 sessions - Query with timestamp index - Verify performance <100ms
4. Test Schema Migration Placeholder - Verify migrations.ts exists - Verify applyMigrations() is callable - Verify no errors thrown (empty implementation)
**Coverage Verification:**
After all tests written: 1. Run: npm run test -- --coverage 2. Verify 100% coverage for: - db.ts - schemas.ts - localStorage.ts - migrations.ts 3. Check coverage/index.html for visual report 4. Ensure no uncovered lines
